name: Invincibility Frames
description: A temporary damage-immunity window that lets an actor ignore eligible hits from specific sources while preserving state priority and readable feedback.
design_notes: Invincibility frames require precise timing and resolver ownership. Define i_frame_source_id, window start and end ticks, priority between roll, spawn protection, hit stun, and cutscene immunity, and which damage types bypass immunity. The system should prevent repeated-hit exploits while making immunity readable through animation, VFX, audio, or UI.
design_purpose: Let players survive through skilled timing, recovery protection, or spawn safety without permanent invulnerability.
Coordinate dodge, parry, hit stun, respawn, and scripted states through one damage resolver rule.
Give combat designers clear tools for making attacks fair without weakening their visible threat.
player_fantasy: The player feels they slipped through danger at the exact moment their action or recovery state allowed it.

parameters.name: i_frame_source_id
parameters.description: State or action that grants temporary immunity.

parameters.name: invulnerability_window_ms
parameters.description: Milliseconds during which eligible damage events are rejected.

parameters.name: i_frame_priority_id
parameters.description: Priority used when multiple immunity sources overlap.

parameters.name: i_frame_start_tick_offset
parameters.description: Simulation tick offset from action start to immunity begin.

parameters.name: i_frame_end_tick_offset
parameters.description: Simulation tick offset from action start to immunity end.

parameters.name: bypass_damage_type_ids
parameters.description: Damage types that can ignore this immunity source.

edge_cases: A lingering area hit overlaps the exact first or last immunity tick and needs inclusive boundary rules.
Two immunity sources overlap with different bypass damage type rules.
A networked client predicts i-frames but the server receives the damage event on a different tick.
Grab, hazard, void, or scripted damage may intentionally bypass ordinary dodge immunity.
common_bugs: Invulnerability is driven only by animation events and changes when animation playback changes.
Damage resolver checks immunity after applying hit stun, knockback, or damage numbers.
Overlapping immunity sources clear each other in the wrong order and leave the actor permanently immune or exposed.
Server and client disagree on the active immunity window because they use wall-clock milliseconds instead of simulation ticks.
balancing_notes: Long immunity windows lower execution pressure but can turn evasive actions into universal defense.
Bypass damage types should be rare and well-telegraphed, or players will stop trusting i-frames.
Recovery immunity can prevent unfair combo hits but should not erase intended pressure loops.
accessibility_notes: Use readable attack telegraphs and optional assist settings for games that rely on narrow immunity timing.
Pair immunity feedback with visual and audio cues, but avoid intense flashing for players with sensitivity settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Store active immunity windows as combat state data with source id, priority, start tick, and end tick. The damage resolver should reject damage_event_id before hit stun, knockback, VFX, or damage numbers are emitted.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep i-frame windows on the combat body or hurtbox component and update them in fixed simulation ticks. Animation signals may request a window, but the damage resolver owns acceptance and bypass rules.
implementation_notes.unreal: Represent i-frames with Gameplay Tags, Gameplay Effects, or a replicated combat state component. Server authority should resolve damage against source priority and bypass damage tags before applying hit reactions.
implementation_notes.web: Store immunity windows in deterministic combat state keyed by actor id and source id. Resolve damage events in tick order, reject eligible hits, and record debug traces for replay or rollback validation.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses dodge-roll invincibility frames as a core defensive timing tool.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses dash immunity and recovery windows in fast combat encounters.
